footer.title: HisarWeb is building a starter that behaves like a product, not a loose template.
footer.description: This sprint lays the groundwork for multilingual delivery, setup-wizard onboarding and a Payload-first content architecture.
footer.rights: All rights reserved. HisarWeb Starter sprint foundation.

home.title: A website starter kit that blends WordPress simplicity with Next.js 15 power.
home.description: We are building a branded multilingual foundation that lets HisarWeb ship faster, deploy more consistently and keep client instances upgradeable later on.

features.title: Why this foundation matters
features.description: The current sprint focuses on scalable building blocks so frontend, backend and operations can keep moving in parallel.

wizard.title: Setup wizard-first
wizard.description: The product flow starts with guided configuration instead of scattered manual setup steps.

locales.title: NL + EN from day one
locales.description: Routing and content structure are multilingual from the start instead of being retrofitted later.

payload.title: Payload-ready backend
payload.description: Collections, globals and helper modules are being introduced as safe skeletons for the next sprint.

delivery.title: Delivery hygiene
delivery.description: README, environment variables, backlog and project structure are prepared for team execution and deployment.

testimonials.title: Sprint outcome
testimonials.description: Not a throwaway demo, but a product-grade foundation the team can extend with confidence.

one.quote: We are replacing template chaos with a product baseline that is faster to sell and easier to maintain.
one.author: Product Owner

two.quote: By placing i18n, backend skeletons and docs early, we avoid expensive rework in later sprints.
two.author: Lead Engineer

three.quote: This foundation is designed to launch multiple client instances consistently and update them safely later on.
three.author: Delivery Lead

cta.title: Ready to move from starter to product
cta.description: The next sprint can now focus safely on real Payload integration, auth wiring and the multi-step setup wizard.

about.title: Built for fast launches without bespoke chaos
about.description: HisarWeb Starter is being shaped as a product foundation for agencies and service businesses that want predictable delivery.

highlights.one: Branded frontend shell with clear content zones
highlights.two: Multilingual route architecture for Dutch and English
highlights.three: Prepared for CMS, auth and update flows

about.ctaDescription: Each sprint delivers usable foundations so sales, onboarding and engineering stay aligned.

blog.title: Content publishing will live here soon
blog.description: This blog space is prepared for editorial listings, filters and rich-text article pages powered by Payload.

highlights.one: Categories and tags as the first content layer
highlights.three: Suitable for thought leadership and release notes

blog.ctaDescription: After the backend sprint, posts, authors and search filters will connect to this template.

contact.title: Turn visitors into discovery calls
contact.description: The contact page is prepared for form flows, company details and later email automation.

highlights.one: Space for form, CTA and service details

contact.ctaDescription: This page will later connect to validation, email templates and CRM handoff.

services.title: Present offers in a modular, scalable way
services.description: Services will later be fed by CMS data so each client type can receive a tailored offer structure.

highlights.one: Grid or list views by sector
highlights.three: Ready for pricing and CTA coupling

services.ctaDescription: The skeleton makes it easy to manage services via Payload in the next sprint.

portfolio.title: Let visual proof build confidence
portfolio.description: Portfolio will later gain filters, project detail pages and media management through Cloudinary and Payload.

highlights.one: Suitable for case studies and galleries

portfolio.ctaDescription: A strong portfolio module helps HisarWeb build deal confidence faster.

pricing.title: Make packages clear, comparable and sellable
pricing.description: The pricing template will later be filled with plans, features and calls to action per audience.

highlights.one: Comparable packages in cards or tables

pricing.ctaDescription: A strong pricing layer accelerates discovery and qualification.

faq.title: Reduce support pressure with clear answers
faq.description: This template will later support categories, search and expandable accordion content.
faq.ctaDescription: FAQ content can later be delivered directly from Payload.

setup.title: From first login to productive website in eight guided steps
setup.description: The wizard is shaped here as a product flow so clients onboard consistently and with confidence.

welcome.title: Welcome & admin account
welcome.description: Initialize owner identity, email and site name.

database.title: Database configuration
database.description: Choose between local SQLite and production PostgreSQL.

industry.title: Industry & sector
industry.description: Activate the right pages and layout defaults per business type.

branding.title: Styling & branding
branding.description: Accent color, logo, typography and dark/light preferences.

modules.title: Module activation
modules.description: Enable blog, contact, services, portfolio, pricing and FAQ.

auth.title: Authentication
auth.description: Control registration, social login and magic links.

languages.title: Languages
languages.description: Pick the default locale and activate additional locales.

confirm.title: Confirm & generate
confirm.description: Review choices and build the first website configuration.

login.title: Admins and editors sign in here
login.description: This temporary flow shows the structure for future auth integration with NextAuth and Payload.

register.title: New user flow based on site configuration
register.description: Registration will later depend on wizard settings and access policy.

forgotPassword.title: Recover access without support friction
forgotPassword.description: This flow will later connect to secure email tokens and Resend templates.
